symptoms.symptom_name: Severe deep throbbing polyarthralgia preventing sleep

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe polyarticular joint pain is the cardinal feature of alphaviral arthritis, though non-specific in isolation.
Polyarticular pain is compatible with an inflammatory arthropathy but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Migratory polyarticular pattern — right knee to left ankle to both wrists

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A migratory polyarticular course involving knees, ankles and wrists is characteristic of alphaviral arthritis.
Septic arthritis is typically monoarticular and fixed; a migratory polyarticular pattern strongly argues against it, though it does not fully exclude the possibility of a superimposed septic joint.
Crystal arthropathy is usually monoarticular or oligoarticular and not classically migratory across this joint distribution.
A transient maculopapular rash accompanying the viraemic prodrome is a well-recognised feature of Barmah Forest and Ross River virus infection preceding the arthralgia.
An antecedent viral exanthem points to a systemic post-viral process rather than a localised joint infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Antecedent multi-day fatigue and malaise (viral prodrome)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prodrome of fatigue and malaise preceding the arthralgia is typical of the viraemic phase of alphaviral infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a single hot, red, dramatically swollen joint and absence of rigors/high fever (negative septic-joint screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a monoarticular hot, erythematous, dramatically swollen joint and of rigors/high fever substantially lowers the probability of septic arthritis, though remote screening cannot exclude it definitively.
A negative hot-joint screen is consistent with a diffuse inflammatory post-viral arthropathy rather than a focal joint infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lives alone with acute functional incapacity — unable to open bottles or prepare food (wrist synovitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Functional incapacity reflects severity and social risk rather than pointing to a specific diagnosis, but it is central to the disposition and support planning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-erythematous mildly effused wrists and left ankle with pain exquisitely worse on active range of motion (synovitis on examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild non-erythematous effusion with pain on active movement is consistent with inflammatory post-viral synovitis; the lack of intense erythema and heat argues against septic arthritis. Requires hands-on joint examination.
Absence of the intense erythema, heat and single-joint dominance typical of sepsis lowers septic arthritis probability on examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Barmah Forest / Ross River virus serology (alphavirus IgM/IgG)

loinc_ref.display: Barmah Forest virus IgM Ab [Presence] in Serum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive alphavirus IgM (with seroconversion of IgG) confirms recent Barmah Forest or Ross River virus infection and is the only means of definitive diagnosis. Cannot be obtained via telehealth chat and does not alter the acute management, which is identical for both viruses.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Post-viral / arbovirus polyarthralgia (Barmah Forest or Ross River virus)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a viral prodrome, an antecedent transient maculopapular rash and an acute migratory polyarticular arthralgia constitutes the archetypal alphaviral arthritis syndrome. No single feature is diagnostic, and serology is required for confirmation, but together they make a benign post-viral arthropathy the leading working diagnosis once septic arthritis has been screened out.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person joint examination to exclude septic arthritis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Hands-on assessment of warmth, erythema, effusion and range of motion is the primary safety step to exclude a hot monoarticular septic joint that cannot be evaluated over chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Barmah Forest and Ross River virus serology (alphavirus IgM/IgG)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only means of confirming the diagnosis and separating the two clinically indistinguishable viruses. It does not change acute management and can be sent via routine pathology; it should not delay same-day assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support septic-arthritis risk stratification alongside clinical examination; markedly raised inflammatory markers with a hot joint would raise concern for infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration and synovial fluid microscopy/culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated on this diffuse migratory polyarticular presentation with a negative hot-joint screen; reserved for a specific joint that is hot, red and dramatically swollen where septic arthritis or crystal arthropathy is suspected. Recommending it routinely here over-investigates a benign post-viral pattern.
